structural_claim: Quantum entanglement imposes fundamental constraints on information and correlations that cannot be understood through single-particle physics

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Quantum entanglement cannot be freely shared among multiple parties, imposing fundamental constraints on multipartite correlations
Quantum states cannot be perfectly duplicated, revealing fundamental constraints on quantum information
Strongly correlated electrons form incompressible quantum fluids that cannot be understood through single-particle physics
Topological order emerges from specific quantum entanglement patterns in the many-body wavefunction
